program: CP 10.3 MCU-native search (2026-07-18 -> 2026-07-20): the OFA-graft family on GAP8
target: GAP8 / Crazyflie AI-deck (int8, 84 KB matched AutoTiler L2, GVSOC sim @175 MHz)

champion.lineage: OFA-graft (winner-v1 backbone arch) + zero-gated topdown neck, DepGraph l2 spec-prune, res 192, 100-ep bare-AdamW recovery (warm gate head, no KD, no recipe-lite)
champion.probe_onnx: models/res192/cand_a5fddcc354bd.onnx (data-free l2 export; cycles are weight-independent + spec-pinned = the trained shape)

baseline.tag: yolo11n-pose @160 (A3 arm, stock recipe, 100-ep, single seed)

verdict: a5fddcc PARETO-DOMINATES the deployed baseline on the GAP8 cycle model: de-noised accuracy 0.6299 >= baseline 0.6227 (EVERY seed >= baseline; mean +0.72 pt), 2.4% FEWER sim cycles (3.0 vs 2.92 FPS), and 2.3x fewer parameters (1.13M vs 2.65M). Survives 3-seed de-noise.
how_it_was_won: recipe-lite FAILED twice (Gate 1 -5 pts @160; the 192-td twin -0.6/-0.8) — does NOT transfer from the dense-recovery regime to the graft
the LEVER was the base architecture: a deeper backbone d=[4,4,2,3,3] + gentle prune (rest 0.45) at res 192 with the topdown neck, surfaced by the wave-2 region-focused cycle screen + proxy
res 160->192 + topdown neck closed the res-screen's -0.088 gap; the base-arch search closed the last 2 pts and flipped it to +0.72
caveats: GAP8 latency is GVSOC SIM cycles (ranking-only, no AI-deck hardware); the ACCURACY is real (CUDA Ultralytics validator, 140-img val, de-noised)
the sim-FPS edge (2.4% cycles) is above the oracle's <1% jitter but slim — the domination rests as much on +accuracy and 2.3x fewer params as on speed
baseline is single-seed (A3); a5fddcc's WORST seed (0.6227) still matches it, so the result is robust to baseline noise
champion WEIGHTS are not persisted (candidate_proxy is an eval runner) — one save-run owed for deployable weights; the arch+spec+ONNX are pinned and reproducible

silicon_measured.method: mcu/board/net_bench on the real Crazyflie AI-deck GAP8; AT_GraphPerf cluster cycles + pi_time wall-clock over CPX; CL 175 MHz; per-inference mean of 20-33 reads (spread <0.03%); int8; STUB weights (16KB-truncated partition, a radio-flash-size fix -- cycles/DMA are data-independent so valid)
silicon_measured.verdict: a5fddcc DOMINATES the baseline by 15.0% on real silicon (55.97M vs 65.83M cyc; 319.8 vs 376.2 ms; +17.6% FPS) -- 6x the sim's 2.4% edge. The baseline runs +10% more cycles on real HW than GVSOC modeled while the graft runs -4% fewer, widening the graft's advantage. CP 10.3's domination HOLDS and STRENGTHENS on silicon.
caveats: STUB weights (data-independent int8 cycles -> valid; a real-weights run owed for a headline absolute; ranking + 15% ratio robust to it)
sim ranking coarse on near-ties: 863c (52.35M cyc) is 6.5% FEWER than a5fddcc on silicon, flipping sim's a5fddcc-dominates-863c into a trade; winner unaffected (accuracy-first pick)
raw-head excludes DFL/anchor/NMS (both families, FC-side); CL 175 MHz reported; still 2.7-3.8 FPS, below the 15-30 FPS racing bar
